elements: 3D surface plot representing a function dependent on two variables
X-axis labeled with ticks and numerical values
Y-axis labeled with ticks and numerical values
Z-axis labeled with ticks and numerical values
Grid overlay on the surface to aid in visualizing topography

interactions.teaches: Orbit the surface to appreciate the 3D form and understand how changes in x and y impact the z values.

interactions.label: Grid Density
interactions.teaches: Adjusting the grid density helps in visualizing finer details of the surface's topography.
Transition through predefined perspectives to highlight specific features of the surface plot.

demo_steps.title: Overview of Function Topography
demo_steps.narration: Initially, observe the general shape of the surface. The different colors indicate the height variations across the surface, with peaks and troughs clearly visible.

demo_steps.title: In-Depth Exploration: Peaks
demo_steps.narration: Focus on a peak. Orbit around the peak to see how it is formed by the underlying function. Notice how the neighboring regions slope downwards.

demo_steps.title: Understanding Valleys
demo_steps.narration: Move to a valley region and observe how it differs from a peak. Understanding this will help in grasping the function's behavior in different areas.

demo_steps.title: Fine-Tuning Topography Details
demo_steps.narration: Adjust the grid density to a higher value to catch subtle changes in surface slope and variation that are not visible with a coarser grid.

demo_steps.title: Comprehensive View
demo_steps.narration: Realize how the interactive elements have given you a deeper understanding of the surface's dimensionality. Recognizing where the surface dips or rises provides insight into the function it represents.

plan.camera_suggestion: Set an initial view that captures the entire surface from a slight above perspective, ensuring the axes and grid are clearly visible without excessive distortion.

elements: grid on the base representing the x-y plane
contours on the surface showing varying heights

interactions.teaches: Allows viewing the surface from different angles to understand its 3D shape and structure

interactions.label: λ
interactions.teaches: Adjusting λ affects the balance between the Laplacian and Gaussian components in the kernel

interactions.label: σ
interactions.teaches: Changing σ controls the width of the Gaussian, affecting the broadness of the surface

demo_steps.title: Exploring Default Parameters
demo_steps.narration: Here you see the default kernel shape with λ=2 and σ=5, representing our early visual system model. This baseline highlights the sharp central peak, typical of the Laplacian’s high-frequency emphasis.

demo_steps.title: Increasing λ Effect
demo_steps.narration: As λ increases, the kernel enhances lower frequencies more, smoothing out the peak and subtly altering the overall shape. This models how our perception balances high and low spatial frequencies.

demo_steps.title: Increasing σ Effect
demo_steps.narration: With a larger σ, the Gaussian component widens, spreading the peak. This shows how broader kernels affect spatial frequency selectivity, influencing the sensitivity model accuracy.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Front-facing perspective view centered to capture the full height and breadth of the surface with uniform grid spacing visible

elements: 3D surface plot of the Laplacian-Gaussian kernel
x-y plane grid base for reference
contour lines on the surface indicating height variation

interactions.teaches: Allows close examination of the kernel's geometric structure from multiple angles, aiding comprehension of the model's shape.

interactions.label: λ
interactions.teaches: Controls the balance between the Laplacian and Gaussian, demonstrating the emphasis on different spatial frequencies.

interactions.label: σ
interactions.teaches: Alters the Gaussian spread, affecting how the kernel covers different spatial extents in visual processing.

demo_steps.title: Exploring Default Parameters
demo_steps.narration: This is the initial shape of the kernel with λ=2 and σ=5. Observe how the model peaks sharply in the center, resembling the high-frequency sensitivity attributed to the Laplacian operation.

demo_steps.title: Effect of Increasing λ
demo_steps.narration: Increasing λ from 2 to 4 results in damping of the high-frequency components, smoothing the central peak. This reflects the balance shift towards integrating more low-frequency information into the model.

demo_steps.title: Effect of Increasing σ
demo_steps.narration: Increasing σ to 7 broadens the Gaussian component, causing the peak to spread more widely. This demonstrates the model's sensitivity to a larger range of spatial frequencies, altering its response profile.

demo_steps.title: Balancing Parameters
demo_steps.narration: With λ=3 and σ=6, witness the kernel's contour integrating both high and low spatial frequencies harmoniously. This visual reflects a more balanced early visual system model, crucial for realistic image analysis.

plan.camera_suggestion: Top-down orthographic view, centered on the kernel surface to mimic the original image orientation as closely as possible, ensuring proper visual correspondence.

inline_constraints.visible_ui: at most 2 compact sliders/toggles, visible near an edge with no filled panel, and must not cover important geometry
inline_constraints.explainers: hover tooltips and click popups only
inline_constraints.framing: match the source crop and whitespace; do not force-fill the iframe if the original has margins
inline_constraints.camera: infer the source camera angle/projection/zoom so the first rendered frame is a drop-in visual replacement

plan.notes: Render the grid in semi-transparent lines to maintain focus on the surface shape. Use smooth animation transitions between demo steps to enhance understanding of parameter effects. Ensure lighting provides clear contour shadowing for depth perception.
